title: Best Buy wants to be the hub for AI-powered hardware like glasses, laptops
description: The tech retailer is looking for growth, as its revenue was essentially flat this past fiscal year from the year before, at almost $42 billion.

title: Future of TV Briefing: OpenX adds attention targeting for CTV ads backed by TVision
description: This week’s Future of TV Briefing looks at a new ad targeting option for CTV advertisers wary of people staring at their phones while an ad airs.

title: AI surfacing is messy: Data shows publisher visibility and traffic often misalign
description: Reports tracking publishers in AI chatbots abound but conflicting rankings and uneven referral traffic reveal the murkiness of AI visibility.

title: The in-house entertainment studio is having its social media team moment
description: Brands are becoming media companies (again). This time they mean it.

title: Today's NYT Mini Crossword Answers for Wednesday, March 11
description: Here are the answers for The New York Times Mini Crossword for March 11.

title: Time-Sensitive Offers in Google Ads
description:
A date-specific ad message does not automatically show, but workarounds can increase its chances.
The post Time-Sensitive Offers in Google Ads appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Fabletics expands into denim to ride casualization trend
description: CEO Adam Goldenberg told Retail Dive in January that shoppers expect athletic brands to offer casual products as apparel trends shift.

title: Kohl’s rules out major store closures despite disappointing Q4
description: The struggling department store is simplifying promotions and adding value-priced merchandise to appeal to its lower- to middle-income customer. But is it going far enough?

title: The Brand House Collective COO exits after less than a year
description: James Schisler resigned from his role while the company is in the process of being acquired by Bed Bath & Beyond Inc.

title: Princess Polly plots next wave of US store openings
description: The apparel brand will open eight stores across Florida, Texas, Minnesota, Tennessee and North Carolina throughout this year and into 2027.

title: Petal & Pup eyes launch with Dillard’s, Von Maur
description: The fashion brand has a successful partnership with Nordstrom and is ready for additional growth.

title: Klaviyo and Shopify announce deeper product integration
description:
Customer relationship management (CRM) platform Klaviyo announced deepened product integration with ecommerce software provider Shopify. Klaviyo said the “expanded interoperability helps enterprises better unify customer data across regions.” In doing so, it said, the integration helps to deliver “consistent, localized experiences worldwide.” Klaviyo framed the integration around merchants expanding worldwide sales and a February 2025 […]
The post Klaviyo and Shopify announce deeper product integration appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Kohl’s leans further into digital sales, Q4 revenue drops
description:
With ecommerce accounting for more than a third of total sales during Kohl’s Corp.’s fiscal Q4 2025, executives shared how the retailer is upgrading its digital infrastructure to improve profit margins, search and discoverability. CEO Michael Bender told analysts on the retailer’s quarterly earnings call that Kohl’s has “an opportunity to modernize” its capabilities and […]
The post Kohl’s leans further into digital sales, Q4 revenue drops appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Agentic commerce faces reality check in B2B ecommerce
description:
The early excitement surrounding agentic commerce — the idea that artificial intelligence (AI) agents can search for products, compare options and complete purchases on behalf of buyers — is encountering a reality check as major technology companies refine their strategies. Recent moves from OpenAI and Amazon suggest the first generation of AI-driven purchasing may evolve […]
The post Agentic commerce faces reality check in B2B ecommerce appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Kimberly-Clark appoints digital-focused executive
description:
Kimberly-Clark Corp. has appointed technology executive Francesco Tinto as chief information and global business services officer, expanding its leadership team as the consumer products manufacturer continues to invest in digital operations and shared services. Tinto assumed the role March 9 and reports to Russ Torres, president and chief operating officer. He will also serve on […]
The post Kimberly-Clark appoints digital-focused executive appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.
